objectClass.notes: This authored benchmark targets branching construction and rooted motion, not exact reconstruction or species identification.

reasoning: A rotation-minimizing trunk and six authored branch pivots form a deep attachment hierarchy.
Foliage representation changes from merged opaque leaf solids to six opaque canopy clusters at minimum tier.

The deterministic authored sway preview must move branch transforms while preserving pot, soil, root anchor, colliders, and exact reset; it does not consume environmental forcing or claim wind dynamics.

specDepthDecision.rationale: Organic attachment continuity, repeated foliage, tier-specific representation, and rigid-pivot rooted articulation require explicit hierarchy and invariants.

definitionOfDone: The design camera reads as a deliberately trained miniature tree with pot, soil, radial root flare, tapered trunk, asymmetrical branch pads, and visible negative spaces.
Every branch begins at a named root socket, ends at a named tip socket, and rotates from its authored branch-root pivot without claiming continuous bending deformation.
Full, budgeted, and minimum tiers reduce geometry while retaining all protected semantic and physics-authoring identities.

featureGroups.name: Pot, soil, and radial root base
qualityCriteria: The oval pot, soil surface, foot, rim, and embedded root flare establish stable scale and support.

featureGroups.name: Oriented trunk and branch hierarchy
qualityCriteria: Rotation-minimizing rings preserve taper and attachment continuity through one trunk, three primary branches, and three secondary branches.

featureGroups.name: Opaque foliage pads
qualityCriteria: Overlapping asymmetric pads retain crown readability without alpha-sorted fragments; minimum uses six opaque clusters.

featureGroups.name: Rooted authored sway preview
qualityCriteria: Action-ready diagnostic motion rotates rigid branch-root pivots, keeps the root anchor fixed, shares color/shadow transforms, and resets exactly without claiming continuous deformation or environmental wind physics.

featureGroups.name: Ceramic, soil, bark, and foliage response
qualityCriteria: Distinct physical-response zones remain readable under neutral and grazing light.

antiShallowSpecRules: Reject a cylinder trunk with floating branch cylinders.
Reject alpha-card foliage or one undifferentiated canopy sphere at full and budgeted tiers.
Reject diagnostic sway that moves the pot, root anchor, or independent shadow geometry, or that is presented as a production wind solver.
Reject tier changes that alter protected semantic or construction-input IDs.
Reject solver, species-accuracy, or visual-acceptance claims without corresponding evidence.

authoringInstruction: Use this assessment as an authored benchmark contract. It does not constitute reference-image, species-accuracy, or visual-acceptance evidence.
